APPEARANCES: Atty. ALISON ROADES-BROWN, ESQ. of Murphy, Logan, Bardwell (Tel: 707-257-8100) appearing telephonically via CourtCall for the plaintiff C.R. Buildworks, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter is submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Jonathan Tunney's demurrer to the first through fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint alleged against the Trust, Christine Tunney individually and as trustee, and himself as trustee is sustained without leave to amend as to the Trust and overruled in all other respects. While it is technically correct that Mr. Tunney may not bring a demurrer on behalf of others, it is also true that the Trust may not be sued. It serves no useful purpose to overrule the demurrer as to the Trust. Liberally construed, the first amended complaint adequately alleges that Mr. Tunney entered into the contract on behalf of himself and his wife in their individual capacities and as trustees. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/HK)
